lines.line: # Early Career and Relocation to Connecticut

> Valeria talks about completing her master's and starting her career in social work. She moved to Connecticut to pursue better opportunities while balancing life as a young mother. She also discusses the start of her role at a halfway house for adolescent boys.
** career, social work, Connecticut, young mother, halfway house **
keywords: ** career, social work, Connecticut, young mother, halfway house **

lines.line: > Valeria shares her pivot back towards her initial interest in law, driven by her background in political science and history. She enrolled at the University of Connecticut for law school while managing her responsibilities as a director of community-based programs.
** law school, University of Connecticut, political science, career transition **

story.title: Valeria Caldwell Oral History.WAV
story.description: The interview with Valeria Caldwell covers her background, education, and career in social work and law. Valeria, originally from Hopkinsville, Kentucky, discusses her academic journey from Kentucky State University to the Kent School of Social Work at the University of Louisville. She shares her experiences balancing motherhood and career aspirations and her eventual move to Connecticut, where she continued practicing social work. Valeria later pursued her interest in law, attending the University of Connecticut Law School. The transition and aspirations both align with and differ from her parents' dreams, highlighting her determination and career progression.
